data.question: Is the R amplitude in lead aVL greater than 1.1mV? Note that R amplitude is the amplitude of the positive deflection in the QRS complex, measured from the baseline to the highest point of the QRS complex.
In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the R amplitude in lead aVL? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured R amplitude fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.

Is the sum of the S amplitude in lead V1 and the R amplitude in lead V5 or V6 greater than 3.5mV? Note that S amplitude is the amplitude of the negative deflection in the QRS complex, measured from the baseline to the lowest point of the QRS complex.
In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the S amplitude in lead V1? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured S amplitude fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.
Which of the following leads show the larger R amplitude? Select the lead from the options below.
In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the R amplitude in the selected lead? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured R amplitude fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.

Does the Cornell Product calculated on this ECG exceed 0.244 mV·s? Note that the Cornell Product is calculated as the sum of the S amplitude in lead V3 and the R amplitude in lead aVL, multiplied by the QRS duration in seconds.
In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the S amplitude in lead V3? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured S amplitude fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.
In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave to measure the R amplitude in lead aVL? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured R amplitude fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS duration fall into? The ranges are inclusive of the lower bound but exclusive of the upper bound.
Does this ECG satisfy at least two of the three voltage criteria for LVH?
